home.heroTag: #1 Soffit & Fascia Specialist in Central Florida
home.heroSubtitle: Holes in your soffit? Panels falling off? Animals getting in? We are the only company in Central Florida that does nothing but soffit and fascia. No roofing. No gutters. No painting. Just this, every day, done right.
home.servicesSubtitle: Whether your soffit is damaged, falling apart, or doesn't exist yet, we handle it. Repairs, full replacement, and new construction installs.
home.service1Desc: Storm ripped a panel off? Dented fascia? Animals chewing through? We seal it, fix it, and reinforce it so it doesn't happen again.
home.service1Bullet3: Seal gaps to prevent pest entry
home.service2Desc: When your old soffit is beyond repair, sagging, rotted, or just ugly, we strip everything off and install a brand-new system. Aluminum or vinyl, your call.
home.service3Desc: Building a house? Your builder probably treats soffit as an afterthought. We don't. IRC 806 compliant ventilation, inspection-ready documentation, coordinated with your build timeline.
home.customSolutionDesc: Send us a photo of your soffit and we'll tell you exactly what it needs. Free, no commitment, no sales pitch.
home.step1Desc: Text us photos of your soffit or call (407) 715-1790. We assess the situation and give you a straight answer, usually the same day.
home.step2Desc: Clear pricing, material options, and a timeline you can count on. No hidden fees, no surprises.
home.step3Desc: Our crew shows up on time, works clean, and leaves your property spotless. Most jobs done in 1-3 days.

home.whyChooseSubtitle: We are the only company in Central Florida that does nothing but soffit and fascia. No roofing side jobs. No gutter add-ons. Just this, every day. That focus is why our work is better.

home.whyMaterialsDesc: Aluminum and vinyl rated for Florida's heat, humidity, and hurricanes. Sourced from ABC Supply, America's largest distributor.
home.whySatisfactionDesc: We don't leave until you're happy. If something's off, we come back and fix it. No excuses.
home.whyAbcDesc: No roofing, no gutters, no painting. Just soffit and fascia, every day, every job. That focus is why our work is better than a general contractor's.
home.pestWarningTitle: That Open Soffit? It's an Invitation.
home.pestWarningSubtitle: Every hole in your soffit is an open door for animals that will destroy your home from the inside out.
home.pest1Desc: Rats and squirrels chew through electrical wires in your attic. One spark and you have a real problem.
home.pest2Desc: Animals soil your insulation with urine and droppings. The removal and replacement costs thousands.
home.pest3Desc: Decaying matter and droppings spread bacteria and toxic odors through your vents into your living space.
home.pest4Desc: Nesting animals chew through wood framing and compromise your home's structure. The longer you wait, the worse it gets.
home.pestDontWait: Every day that hole stays open, something is getting in.
home.reviewsSubtitle: We build our reputation one house at a time. Every job is a chance to earn a 5-star review, and we take that seriously. Check our latest work on Google.

calculator.title: Soffit & Fascia Cost Calculator
calculator.subtitle: Get an instant estimate for your project
calculator.disclaimer: This is an estimate only. Final price may vary based on site conditions.
calculator.introSubtitle: Get a transparent, instant estimate for your soffit & fascia project.
calculator.typeSelectSubtitle: Select the option that best describes your needs.
calculator.successMsg: Our team will review your photos and call you back with a repair estimate — usually within a few hours.
calculator.showDamageSubtitle: Upload 2–5 photos of the damaged soffit or fascia. More angles = better estimate.
calculator.photosHelpText: Include a close-up of the damage, a wide shot showing the full area, and a photo from each affected side.
calculator.tapUploadDesc: Different angles of the damage — soffit, fascia, roof edge.
calculator.photosReady: {count} photos selected — great! You're ready to continue.
calculator.photosNeedMore: Just 1 photo so far. Add at least one more for a better quote.
calculator.repairStep2Subtitle: Photos received. Fill in your details and our team will call you back with a precise repair estimate — usually within a few hours.
calculator.placeholderDescription: Animal intrusion, storm damage, rotting wood, falling soffit...?
calculator.repairError: ⚠ Could not send. Please try again or call us at (407) 715-1790.
calculator.repairCallbackNote: We call back within a few hours · No commitment needed
calculator.guideTitle: How to measure before using the sliders
calculator.guideStep1Desc: Walk the outside perimeter of your home with a tape measure and add up the length of every side that has soffit or fascia. Tip: Google Maps "Measure Distance" gives a quick estimate.
calculator.guideStep1Caption: Add up the length along each side of the roof perimeter. Example: 80 ft front + 60 ft back + 50 ft each side = 240 LF total.
calculator.guideStep2Desc: This is the horizontal distance from the exterior wall out to the very edge of the roof. Measure it from outside. Common sizes: 12" (standard), 16–24" (moderate), 30–36" (deep/custom).
calculator.guideStep2Caption: Measure from the wall outward to the drip edge of the roof. Different sides of the house may have different overhangs — measure each one.
calculator.guideStep3Desc: Each 90° corner adds extra trim work. A simple box house has 4 corners. An L-shaped house has 6. Mention the count in your quote request so we don't underestimate trim labor.
calculator.guideStep3Caption: Count every outward-facing corner of the roofline. Rectangle = 4 corners. L-shape = 6. U-shape = 8.
calculator.guideTipBold: Not sure about the exact size?
calculator.guideTipText: No problem. Use an estimate now — our team corrects it for free during the on-site visit.
calculator.ovhWarning: ⚠ Overhangs above 36" require an on-site structural review before we can quote online.
calculator.resultSubtitle: Transparent pricing for your Orlando property.
calculator.blockedDesc: Your overhang depth (>36") requires structural wood reinforcement before aluminum wrapping. Contact us for a free on-site assessment.
calculator.resultDisclaimer: * Automated estimate based on your inputs. This is not a contract or binding quote. Final price confirmed after our free on-site visit. All services subject to our
calculator.lockQuote: Lock This Quote & Book a Visit

financing.faq1Q: What is a soft credit pull and how is it different from a hard pull?
financing.faq1A: A soft pull lets you see your rate options without any impact on your credit score — you can check 30+ offers and nothing shows up on your report. A hard pull only happens when you formally accept a specific loan offer. Acorn Finance only does a soft pull during the rate-check phase.
financing.faq2Q: How fast do I get pre-approved?
financing.faq2A: Pre-approval takes about 60 seconds after you fill out the short form. Once you accept an offer, funding typically arrives in your account within 24–48 hours.
financing.faq3Q: My credit score isn't great. Can I still get financing?
financing.faq3A: Possibly, yes. Acorn Finance works with 30+ lending partners, some of which accept FICO scores starting at 550. There's no cost to check — the worst that can happen is you don't qualify, and we explore other options together.
financing.faq4Q: What's the minimum and maximum loan amount?
financing.faq4A: Minimum is $1,000. Maximum is $100,000. Most Bubbles Enterprise projects fall between $1,500 and $12,000 depending on home size, linear feet, and scope of work.
financing.faq5Q: Is Bubbles Enterprise the lender? Who do I actually owe money to?
financing.faq5A: Bubbles Enterprise is not a lender. We connect you to Acorn Finance, which works with independent banks and lending partners. You'll pay them directly — we receive our payment after the job is complete, same as any normal project.
financing.faq6Q: What if I'm not approved by any lender?
financing.faq6A2: Our team reviews each case personally and may be able to arrange a direct payment schedule for qualified customers.
financing.faq7Q: Do I need to be the homeowner to apply?
financing.faq7A: Yes. Financing for home improvement projects requires the applicant to be the legal owner of the property where the work will be done.
financing.faq8Q: Can I pay off the loan early without a penalty?
financing.faq8A: Most lenders in the Acorn network have no prepayment penalties, meaning you can pay off your loan early without extra fees. Confirm the specific terms when you review your offer — it will be clearly stated before you accept.
financing.faq9Q: I speak Spanish. Can someone help me in Spanish?
financing.faq9WhatsappMsg: Hello, I have questions about financing.
financing.faq9A2: or call (407) 715-1790. Our team is bilingual (English/Spanish) and will walk you through the entire process.
financing.ctaFinalSubtitle: Check your rate now. No commitment. No credit impact. Takes 60 seconds.
financing.ctaFinalWhatsappMsg: Hi Bubbles team, I have a question about financing my soffit project.
financing.exitBody: The form can feel like a lot. Our team answers personally — in English or Spanish — and walks you through it in minutes.

faqPage.metaTitle: FAQ — Soffit & Fascia Questions | Bubbles Enterprise Orlando
faqPage.metaDesc: Common questions about soffit and fascia repair, installation, costs, warranty, rotten wood policy, and our process in Orlando, FL. Expert answers from Bubbles Enterprise.
faqPage.metaOgTitle: FAQ — Soffit & Fascia Questions | Bubbles Enterprise
faqPage.metaOgDesc: Get answers about soffit repair, material choices, pricing, warranty, cancellation policy, and our process in Orlando.
faqPage.heroSubtitle: Honest answers about soffit, fascia, costs, material policies, and our process — no fluff.
faqPage.cat1q1: What is the difference between soffit and fascia?
faqPage.cat1a1: Soffit is the exposed surface beneath the overhanging section of a roof eave — it bridges the gap between your siding and the roofline. Fascia is the vertical finishing edge connected to the ends of the rafters, where the gutter is attached. Together, they protect your home's exterior structure and provide essential attic ventilation.
faqPage.cat1q2: Why is soffit ventilation so important in Florida?
faqPage.cat1a2: Proper soffit ventilation allows fresh air to flow into your attic while hot air escapes through ridge vents. In Florida's heat and humidity, without this airflow you get moisture buildup that causes mold, wood rot, and premature roof shingle failure. Good ventilation can reduce your cooling costs and extend your roof's lifespan significantly.
faqPage.cat1q3: Can you repair animal damage to soffits?
faqPage.cat1a3: Absolutely. Squirrels, raccoons, rats, and birds are common in Florida attics. We don't just patch the hole — we repair the damaged section and reinforce the area with durable materials to prevent future entry. Address these issues immediately to avoid electrical fire risks and insulation contamination.
faqPage.cat2a1: We primarily use high-quality aluminum and vinyl. Aluminum is durable, fire-resistant, low-maintenance, and lasts 20–30 years. Vinyl is cost-effective, rot-resistant, and ideal for high-moisture environments — lifespan around 10 years. We'll recommend the right material based on your home's architecture and budget.
faqPage.cat2q2: How much does soffit installation cost?
faqPage.cat2a2: Cost depends on linear footage, overhang depth, material choice (aluminum vs. vinyl), and whether it's new construction or a remove-and-replace job. Use our online calculator for an instant estimate, or contact us for a free on-site quote. Typical ranges: $6/LF for 1-story remove & replace, $7/LF for 2-story.
faqPage.cat2q3: How long does a soffit and fascia installation take?
faqPage.cat2a3: Most standard single-story homes take 1–2 days. Two-story homes and full Remove & Replace projects typically take 2–3 days. New construction is coordinated with your build timeline. We always give you a specific timeline before starting.
faqPage.cat3q1: What happens if rotten wood is found during installation?
faqPage.cat3a1: If we remove the old material and discover rotten wood in the sub-fascia or trusses, we stop work in that area immediately and notify you. We do not perform structural framing repairs (we don't hold a framing license). You'll need to hire a licensed carpenter to replace the wood before we continue. This is always communicated up front — no surprise charges.
faqPage.cat3q2: Will my gutters be removed and reinstalled?
faqPage.cat3a2: If gutters need to be removed to install new fascia, the old gutters will NOT be reinstalled. They deform and warp upon removal, making a proper seal impossible. You'll need to hire a gutter company to install a new system after our work is complete. We only perform Soffit and Fascia.
faqPage.cat3q3: What about my outdoor lights, satellites, or security cameras?
faqPage.cat3a3: We remove and reinstall soffit lights as a courtesy of access. However, if a fixture is old and breaks in our hands, or if wiring is brittle and fails, we are not responsible for replacement. Satellite dishes and cameras are returned to their position, but we don't guarantee signal alignment — the customer's provider handles recalibration.
faqPage.cat3q4: How do you handle cleanup after the job?
faqPage.cat3a4: Our cleanup is standard job-site quality: full sweeping and a magnetic sweep for nails. If thermal insulation falls from your attic during soffit replacement, we clean the bulk with a blower. Fine particles in grass or rocks are unavoidable in this type of renovation and do not constitute mess left behind.
faqPage.cat4a1: We offer a 1-Year Workmanship Warranty on all installations. This covers any piece that comes loose, unclips, or falls due to an installation error. It does NOT cover damage from storms/hurricanes ('Acts of God'), animals chewing through the material, or damage caused by third parties modifying the work after us.
faqPage.cat4a2: The final balance is due immediately upon job completion and walkthrough — before we leave the property. Deposits are required prior to scheduling. We accept check, Zelle, or cash. The company retains a purchase-money security interest in installed materials per Chapter 713, Florida Statutes, until full payment is received.
faqPage.cat4a3: If you cancel after materials have been purchased, a fee of 20% of the deposit value will be charged to cover the supplier's restocking fee and our project preparation costs. This fee is disclosed upfront and represents liquidated damages as agreed upon at booking.
faqPage.cat4a4: Yes. We provide free, no-obligation on-site estimates for all soffit and fascia projects in Orlando and surrounding Central Florida areas. We visit your property, assess the current condition, and provide a transparent quote with no hidden fees. You can also get an instant online estimate using our calculator.
faqPage.cat5a1: We serve the entire Greater Orlando area and Central Florida, including Orlando, Winter Park, Kissimmee, Altamonte Springs, Clermont, Sanford, Oviedo, Lake Mary, Apopka, and Maitland. We also travel to South Florida for larger projects. If you're unsure we cover your area, just call!
faqPage.stillQSubtitle: We're here to help. No pressure, no sales pitch.

about.title: About Bubbles Enterprise | Orlando's Soffit & Fascia Specialist
about.subtitle: The only company in Central Florida that does nothing but soffit and fascia. That's why builders trust us and homeowners call us by name.
about.heroBadge: Soffit & Fascia. That's All We Do.
about.heroSubtitle: Most contractors do soffit as an afterthought between roofing jobs and painting gigs. We built an entire company around it. Every crew member, every tool, every process is designed for one thing: soffit and fascia done right.
about.storyP1: was founded in Central Florida because our team saw a problem: every contractor in town treats soffit as a side job. Rush it, slap it on, move to the next house. The result? Panels falling off within years. Failed inspections. Animals in the attic. Homeowners calling someone else to fix the first guy's mess.
about.storyP2: So we built a company that does nothing else. No roofing. No gutters. No painting. Just soffit and fascia, every day, on every job. That focus means our crew has seen every type of soffit problem Florida can throw at a house, and we've solved all of them.
about.storyP3: That's why KB Home and DR Horton put us on their preferred list. And why 500+ homeowners across Central Florida call us by name. When you only do one thing, you get very good at it.
about.storyGalleryLink: See the results in our gallery
about.missionText: Protect Orlando homes with soffit and fascia that lasts decades. Premium materials, expert installation, zero shortcuts.
about.promiseText: We don't leave until it's perfect. Every project backed by 100% satisfaction guarantee and material warranties up to 30 years. If something goes wrong, we come back and fix it. Period.
about.quoteText: We don't cut corners. We don't rush. And we don't do anything except soffit and fascia. That's why our work lasts.

about.timeline2Desc: Word gets around. 100+ homeowners in Orlando choose us. 5-star reviews start piling up.
about.timeline3Desc: KB Home and DR Horton select us as their go-to soffit contractor across Central Florida. Zero callbacks.
about.timeline4Desc: 500+ homes protected. ABC Supply authorized dealer. NAHB Standards certified. Still doing one thing.
about.timeline5Desc: The most specialized soffit and fascia company in Central Florida. Setting the standard for the entire region.
about.value1Desc: Every panel aligned. Every vent calculated. Every screw placed where it belongs. We treat your house like it's ours.
about.value2Desc: You'll know what's happening, when it's happening, and what it costs before we start. No surprises. No unanswered calls.
about.value3Desc: Most projects done in 1-3 days. We don't drag jobs out. Our crew shows up, works clean, and finishes when we say we will.

about.value6Desc: Proper ventilation designed into every install. Your attic stays cool, your energy bills drop, and your roof lasts longer.

privacy.metaDesc: Privacy policy for Bubbles Enterprise LLC — how we collect, use, and protect your personal information. We never sell your data. Orlando, FL.
privacy.metaOgDesc: We collect only what's needed to serve you. No data selling, no third-party marketing. Read our full privacy policy.
privacy.heroSubtitle: Bubbles Enterprise LLC — we collect only what's needed, protect it with care, and never sell it.
privacy.sum1Desc: Your info never goes to third-party marketers.
privacy.sum2Desc: HTTPS + multi-layer spam protection on every form.
privacy.sum3Desc: Data used only to serve your project — nothing else.
privacy.ctaSubtitle: Questions about how we handle your data? Contact us directly.
privacy.collectIntro: We collect only information you explicitly provide when:
privacy.collectItem1: name, phone, email, property address, and project details (linear footage, overhang depth, material preference).
privacy.collectItem2: anonymous measurement data used only to generate your estimate on-screen. Not stored on our servers.
privacy.collectItem3: call records, text messages, or emails you initiate.
privacy.collectNote: We do not require account creation. You are never required to provide information beyond what is needed to complete a quote or service request.
privacy.useItem1: Prepare and deliver your soffit/fascia estimate
privacy.useItem3: Send job confirmation, pre-visit notices, and payment receipts
privacy.useItem4: Follow up on open quotes or active service requests
privacy.sharingPromise: We have never sold, rented, or traded your personal information to any third-party marketing company — and we never will.
privacy.sharingIntro: We may share information only in these limited circumstances:
privacy.sharingItem1: to forward your contact form to our team. They do not use your data for marketing.
privacy.sharingItem2: only when required by a valid court order or Florida law.
privacy.cookiesIntro: Our website uses Google Analytics 4 and Microsoft Clarity for traffic analysis and user experience improvement. We do not use:
privacy.cookiesNo1: Facebook Pixel or behavioral retargeting cookies
privacy.cookiesLocal: Your browser may store minimal localStorage data for locale preferences. This data never leaves your device.
privacy.securityItem1: Our website enforces HTTPS encryption on all pages
privacy.securityItem2: Contact form submissions are protected by rate limiting and multi-layer spam detection
privacy.securityItem3: HTTP security headers: X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, Permissions-Policy
privacy.securityItem4: No customer data is stored in databases — we operate via email and direct communication
privacy.retentionBody: We retain contact information and service records for as long as needed to fulfill the contracted work and meet Florida legal requirements (typically 3 years for contractor records).
privacy.retentionDeleteBold: To request deletion of your information,
privacy.retentionDeleteTime: We will process deletion requests within 30 days.
privacy.contactBody: For any privacy-related questions or data requests, contact us directly:

repairs.metaTitle: Soffit & Fascia Repair Services Orlando | Bubbles Enterprise
repairs.metaDesc: Fast soffit and fascia repairs in Orlando: storm damage, animal intrusion, water damage, pest gaps. Same-week service. Licensed & Insured. Free estimates. (407) 715-1790.
repairs.metaOgTitle: Soffit & Fascia Repair | Bubbles Enterprise Orlando
repairs.metaOgDesc: Fast, honest soffit repairs in Orlando. Emergency service available. 60-80% cheaper than full replacement for isolated damage.
repairs.heroTitle2: We Close It Before It Gets Worse.
repairs.heroSubtitle: Open holes where animals can get in. A panel ripped off by last night's storm. Dented fascia. Water stains spreading. If you don't seal it now, it gets worse every single day — and we fix it fast.
repairs.signsTitle: Is This Happening to Your Home?
repairs.signsSubtitle: If you see any of these, it's not going away on its own. Florida makes it worse — fast.
repairs.damage1Body: You can literally see the damage — panels hanging, fascia dented by a branch, gaps open to the sky. Every day that hole stays open, something's getting in.
repairs.damage2Body: Brown stains on the soffit? Soft spots on the fascia? That's moisture working its way through. After one Florida rainy season, this turns into mold fast.
repairs.damage3Title: Your Attic Is Way Too Hot
repairs.damage3Body: If your AC runs nonstop and upstairs is always hot, your soffit vents might be blocked or crushed. Bad ventilation = higher electric bills and a shorter roof lifespan.
repairs.damage4Body: Squirrels, raccoons, birds, rats — they all enter through soffit gaps. Once inside, they nest, breed, and cause thousands in damage. We seal the gap so nothing gets in. Animal removal is done by a wildlife company before we close it up.
repairs.damage5Body: Florida's UV is brutal. If your soffit looks washed out, cracked, or is peeling — that's not just cosmetic. The material is failing and it'll only get worse.
repairs.damage6Body: Panels pulling away? Gaps where the soffit meets the fascia? Fasteners popping out? Someone cut corners the first time. We fix what they got wrong.
repairs.benefitsTitle: Why Repair — Not Replace the Whole Thing?
repairs.benefitsSubtitle: If the damage is localized, repair is the smart move. Here's why.
repairs.benefit1Body: Why replace the entire system when only a section is damaged? Targeted repair saves you serious money — and we only fix what actually needs fixing.
repairs.benefit2Body: Most repairs are done in 1-2 days. You call, we come out, we fix it. No waiting around for weeks like with a full replacement project.
repairs.benefit3Body: That small gap isn't staying small. In Florida, moisture and animals work fast. A repair today prevents a full replacement next year.
repairs.benefit4Body: Every repair comes with our 1-year workmanship guarantee in writing. If it comes loose because of us, we come back free.
repairs.ctaSubtitle: Every day it stays open, water gets in, animals find it, and UV breaks down more material. Text us a photo or call — we'll tell you exactly what needs sealing and what it'll cost. No pressure, no obligation.
repairs.ctaSubtext: Free inspection. Honest assessment. No upselling.

services.metaTitle: Soffit & Fascia Services Orlando | Bubbles Enterprise
services.metaDesc: Expert soffit and fascia services in Orlando: repairs, remove & replace, and new construction. Aluminum & vinyl specialists. Free estimates. Call (407) 715-1790.
services.metaOgTitle: Soffit & Fascia Services | Bubbles Enterprise Orlando
services.metaOgDesc: Repairs, Remove & Replace, and New Construction soffit services across Central Florida.
services.heroSubtitle: Animals in your attic? Panels falling off? Water damage behind the fascia? We're the only crew in Orlando that does nothing but soffit and fascia — and we do it better than anyone.
services.specialtyDesc: Most contractors do soffit "on the side" between roofing and painting jobs. We don't. Soffit and fascia is all we do — every day, every job. No roofing, no gutters, no side work. That's why we're faster, cleaner, and more precise than any general contractor.
services.servicesTitle: Three Ways We Solve Your Problem
services.servicesSubtitle: Pick the one that fits. We'll handle the rest.
services.svc1Desc: Got a hole where raccoons are getting in? Storm ripped off a panel? We patch, replace, and restore — usually within the same week you call us.
services.svc1f2: Seal open holes — stop animals today
services.svc2Desc: When your soffit is beyond repair — warped, rotted, or 20+ years old — we strip it all and install a brand-new system. Aluminum or vinyl, your pick.
services.svc2f4: Fascia wrap included at no extra cost
services.svc3Desc: Building a new home or addition? We work directly with your GC to install soffit and fascia on schedule, to code, first time. No callbacks.
services.materialsSubtitle: We specialize in two premium soffit materials
services.processSubtitle: From call to completion in 4 simple steps — no surprises, no hidden fees
services.proc1Body: Text us a few photos or call (407) 715-1790. We'll assess your soffit for free — no visit needed for most quotes.
services.proc2Body: You'll receive a written quote with every cost broken down. No hidden fees, no "oh by the way" charges on install day.
services.proc3Body: Our crew shows up on time, installs to code, and cleans up before we leave. Most jobs done in 1-3 days.
services.proc4Body: We walk the job with you before we leave. If anything isn't right, we fix it on the spot. 1-year labor warranty included.
services.calcTitle: Curious what your project will cost?
services.calcSubtitle: Get an instant ballpark in under 60 seconds — no email, no phone call required.
services.faqTitle: Got Questions? We Get These a Lot.
services.faqSubtitle: Real answers from the crew that does this every day
services.faq1q: How long will my project take?
services.faq1a: Most homes are done in 1-3 days. A simple repair? Often the same day. A full Remove & Replace on a two-story? About 3 days. We'll give you the exact timeline in your quote so there are no surprises.
services.faq2q: Should I go with aluminum or vinyl soffit?
services.faq2a: Aluminum lasts 20-30 years, is fire-resistant, and handles Florida's UV and hurricanes better. Vinyl costs less upfront and works great for budget projects or additions. Most Orlando homeowners choose aluminum for the long haul — but we'll help you pick what makes sense for your home and budget.
services.faq3a: Every installation comes with our 1-year workmanship guarantee — if anything comes loose or falls due to our installation, we come back and fix it free. Aluminum soffit also carries manufacturer warranties up to 30 years. Storm damage and animal tampering are excluded (those are separate repair calls).
services.faq4q: How much does soffit replacement cost in Orlando?
services.faq4a: It depends on material, linear footage, and whether you're repairing or replacing. Every home is different — that's why we offer free quotes with no obligation. Use our online calculator for an instant ballpark, or text us photos and we'll get you an exact number the same day.
services.faq5q: Why does my soffit need ventilation?
services.faq5a: Florida's heat and humidity trap moisture in your attic. Without proper soffit ventilation, you get mold, wood rot, higher AC bills, and a shortened roof lifespan. Properly vented soffit can cut your cooling costs by 15-25%. We design ventilation into every install — it's not an add-on, it's standard.
services.faq6q: Do you only do soffit? What about gutters and roofing?
services.faq6a: Just soffit and fascia — that's it. We don't do roofing, gutters, painting, or anything else. Why? Because focus = quality. Every job we've ever done is soffit. That's why we're faster, cleaner, and more precise than a general contractor who does soffit "on the side."
services.faq7q: What happens if you find rotten wood behind my soffit?
services.faq7a: We stop work in that area immediately and show you the damage. We don't hold a framing license, so you'll need a licensed carpenter to replace the rotten wood before we continue. We won't install new soffit over bad structure — it would just fail again. We'll coordinate timing so your project stays on track.
services.faq8a: Yes. We partner with Acorn Finance, which connects you to 30+ lenders. Approval takes minutes, credit scores as low as 550 qualify, and terms go up to 144 months. Check our financing page for details.
services.ctaSubtitle: That damaged soffit isn't fixing itself — and every week you wait, animals, moisture, and UV are making it worse. Get a free quote today and we'll have a crew out this week.
services.ctaSubtext: Free inspection. No pressure. No obligation.

terms.metaTitle: Terms of Service | Bubbles Enterprise Soffit
terms.metaDesc: Full terms of service for Bubbles Enterprise LLC — soffit and fascia installation in Orlando, FL. Covers warranty, rotten wood policy, gutters, payment, and Florida statutory notices.
terms.metaOgDesc: Complete service terms, warranty information, and Florida mandatory notices for Bubbles Enterprise LLC.
terms.heroSubtitle: Bubbles Enterprise LLC — Florida-licensed soffit & fascia contractor. Last updated: February 2026.
terms.ctaSubtitle: We're happy to clarify anything before you book.
terms.c1Body: Our quote covers installation over solid wood structure. If, upon removing the old material, we find rotten wood in the structure (sub-fascia or trusses), we will stop work in that area immediately.
terms.c1Note: We do NOT perform structural framing repairs, as we do not hold a framing license. You will need to hire a licensed carpenter to replace the wood before we can continue installation.
terms.c2Body: If your home has gutters that must be removed to install new fascia, old gutters will NOT be reinstalled. They deform and warp upon removal, making it impossible to guarantee a proper seal or slope afterward.
terms.c2Note: You will need to hire a specialized gutter company to install a new system after our work is complete. Bubbles Enterprise performs Soffit and Fascia only.
terms.c3Lights: We remove and reinstall soffit lights as an access courtesy. If a fixture is old and breaks in our hands, or if wiring is brittle and fails when moved, we are not responsible for replacement.
terms.c3Cameras: We remove them to work and reinstall in place, but we do not guarantee signal alignment. If Starlink, TV, or camera signal is lost, the customer must call the provider's technician for recalibration.
terms.c4Body: We are not responsible for phone, internet, or alarm wires left loose inside the old soffit without conduit that become damaged during removal of old material. Pre-existing loose wiring is the homeowner's responsibility.
terms.c5Body: Our cleanup is standard job-site quality: sweeping and magnetic sweep for nails.
terms.c5Note: If thermal insulation (fiberglass) falls from your attic during soffit replacement, we will clean the excess with a blower. Small particles may remain in grass or rocks — this is unavoidable in this type of renovation and does not constitute "mess left behind."
terms.c6Intro: We offer a 1-Year Workmanship Warranty on all installations.
terms.c6Covered: Any piece that comes loose, unclips, or falls due to installation error.
terms.c6NotCovered: Storms/Hurricanes ("Acts of God"), animal damage (squirrels/rodents), or damage caused by third parties touching the work.
terms.c7Plants: Our work is performed immediately against the walls. Ladders and equipment will inevitably touch surrounding plants. Some branches may be crushed or broken. If you have plants of sentimental value or fragile pots, please remove or protect them before our arrival.
terms.c7Sprinklers: Please mark or notify our installer where irrigation heads are located. If we are not warned and step on a hidden head, we do not cover the repair.
terms.c8Body: Our crew uses a pickup truck and a large trailer. It is the customer's responsibility to ensure a legally permissible parking space for both vehicles. If the HOA or municipality tows or fines our equipment due to lack of a proper spot, the cost is on the customer.
terms.c9Body: The final balance is due immediately upon completion and walkthrough.
terms.c9Note: Bubbles Enterprise LLC retains a purchase-money security interest in the installed materials and reserves the right to file a claim of lien per Chapter 713, Florida Statutes, until full payment is received.
terms.c10Body: If you cancel the service after materials have been purchased, a fee of 20% of the deposit value will be charged. This covers the material restocking fee charged by the supplier plus project preparation costs. This amount constitutes liquidated damages as agreed upon at booking.
terms.c11Venue: Venue for any dispute: Orange County, FL
terms.c11Fees: The prevailing party in any legal dispute is entitled to attorney's fees.
terms.c11Agreement: This document, together with acceptance via text or email, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties.
terms.fn1Body: PAYMENT, UP TO A LIMITED AMOUNT, MAY BE AVAILABLE FROM THE FLORIDA HOMEOWNERS' CONSTRUCTION RECOVERY FUND IF YOU LOSE MONEY ON A PROJECT PERFORMED UNDER CONTRACT, WHERE THE LOSS RESULTS FROM SPECIFIED VIOLATIONS OF FLORIDA LAW BY A LICENSED CONTRACTOR. FOR MORE INFORMATION ABOUT THE RECOVERY FUND AND THE CONDITIONS FOR RECOVERY, CONTACT THE FLORIDA CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY LICENSING BOARD.
terms.fn2Title: Construction Lien Law Notice — Fla. Stat. § 713.015
terms.fn2Body: ACCORDING TO FLORIDA'S CONSTRUCTION LIEN LAW (SECTIONS 713.001-713.37, FLORIDA STATUTES), THOSE WHO WORK ON YOUR PROPERTY OR PROVIDE MATERIALS AND SERVICES AND ARE NOT PAID IN FULL HAVE A RIGHT TO ENFORCE THEIR CLAIM FOR PAYMENT AGAINST YOUR PROPERTY. THIS CLAIM IS KNOWN AS A CONSTRUCTION LIEN. IF YOUR CONTRACTOR OR A SUBCONTRACTOR FAILS TO PAY SUBCONTRACTORS, SUB-SUBCONTRACTORS, OR MATERIAL SUPPLIERS, THOSE PEOPLE WHO ARE NOT PAID MAY LOOK TO YOUR PROPERTY FOR PAYMENT, EVEN IF YOU HAVE ALREADY PAID YOUR CONTRACTOR IN FULL. IF YOU FAIL TO PAY YOUR CONTRACTOR, YOUR CONTRACTOR MAY ALSO HAVE A LIEN ON YOUR PROPERTY. THIS MEANS IF A LIEN IS FILED YOUR PROPERTY COULD BE SOLD AGAINST YOUR WILL TO PAY FOR LABOR, MATERIALS, OR OTHER SERVICES THAT YOUR CONTRACTOR OR A SUBCONTRACTOR MAY HAVE FAILED TO PAY. FLORIDA'S CONSTRUCTION LIEN LAW IS COMPLEX, AND IT IS RECOMMENDED THAT YOU CONSULT AN ATTORNEY.
terms.fn3Body: CHAPTER 558, FLORIDA STATUTES, CONTAINS IMPORTANT REQUIREMENTS YOU MUST FOLLOW BEFORE YOU MAY BRING ANY LEGAL ACTION FOR AN ALLEGED CONSTRUCTION DEFECT. SIXTY DAYS BEFORE YOU BRING ANY LEGAL ACTION, YOU MUST DELIVER TO THE OTHER PARTY TO THIS CONTRACT A WRITTEN NOTICE, REFERRING TO CHAPTER 558, OF ANY CONSTRUCTION CONDITIONS YOU ALLEGE ARE DEFECTIVE AND PROVIDE US THE OPPORTUNITY TO INSPECT THE ALLEGED DEFECTIVE CONDITION AND TO CONSIDER MAKING AN OFFER TO REPAIR OR PAY FOR THE ALLEGED DEFECTIVE CONDITION. YOU ARE NOT OBLIGATED TO ACCEPT ANY OFFER WHICH MAY BE MADE. THERE ARE STRICT DEADLINES AND PROCEDURES UNDER FLORIDA LAW WHICH MUST BE MET AND FOLLOWED TO PROTECT YOUR INTERESTS.

materialsAluminum.metaTitle: Aluminum Soffit Orlando | 20-30 Yr Lifespan | Bubbles
materialsAluminum.metaDesc: Premium aluminum soffit in Orlando FL. Fire-resistant, non-combustible, hurricane-rated, 10 color options, 20-30 year lifespan. Trusted by Florida's top home builders. Free estimate.
materialsAluminum.metaOgTitle: Aluminum Soffit Installation | Bubbles Enterprise Orlando
materialsAluminum.metaOgDesc: The professional choice. Fire-resistant aluminum soffit with 20-30 year lifespan. 10 colors. Free estimate.
materialsAluminum.heroSubtitle: Fire-resistant, hurricane-rated, and maintenance-free for 20-30 years. Trusted by Florida's top builders for a reason.
materialsAluminum.whyDesc: Builders and architects specify aluminum for any project where durability matters. 50-year manufacturer warranty options available. The only non-combustible soffit material.
materialsAluminum.feat4: Low maintenance — won't rot, crack, or warp
materialsAluminum.warrantyDesc: Our aluminum soffit comes with manufacturer warranty up to 30 years, plus our 1-year installation workmanship guarantee.
materialsAluminum.ben1Body: Engineered to withstand Florida hurricane-force winds — standard aluminum soffit rating.
materialsAluminum.ben2Body: Non-combustible material. Won't spread flame, won't fuel a fire. Critical for Florida code.
materialsAluminum.ben3Body: Available in solid and vented (perforated) profiles for maximum attic airflow.
materialsAluminum.ben4Body: 100% moisture resistant. Florida humidity and rain will never cause deterioration.
materialsAluminum.svSubtitle: Choose the right profile for each area of your roof
materialsAluminum.solidDesc: Non-perforated panels for areas where ventilation isn't needed — like garage eaves or architectural details. Provides a smooth, elegant finish that completely seals the exterior.
materialsAluminum.ventedDesc: Precision perforations allow continuous air circulation. Critical for attic health, mold prevention, and energy efficiency in Florida's humid climate.
materialsAluminum.ventedFloridaTip: Essential for Florida: IRC 806 requires proper soffit ventilation to prevent mold and reduce cooling costs by up to 25%.
materialsAluminum.colorsSubtitle: White & Black in stock for immediate installation. Other colors: 1–3 business days.
materialsAluminum.colorsDisclaimer: Colors are digitally represented. We recommend requesting physical samples before your final decision.
materialsAluminum.ctaSubtitle: Durable, beautiful, built to last 20-30 years in Florida's climate. One installation you won't think about for decades.

materialsVinyl.whyDesc: Vinyl delivers excellent value for homeowners who want quality without overpaying. A smart choice combining functionality, color variety, and economy.

materialsHub.metaTitle: Aluminum vs Vinyl Soffit — Materials Guide | Bubbles Enterprise Orlando
materialsHub.metaDesc: Complete materials guide for soffit and fascia in Central Florida. Compare aluminum and vinyl — lifespan, cost, maintenance, hurricane rating, and which to choose.
materialsHub.metaOgTitle: Soffit Materials Guide: Aluminum vs Vinyl | Bubbles Enterprise
materialsHub.metaOgDesc: Compare aluminum and vinyl soffit for Florida homes — lifespan, cost, hurricane resistance, and the right choice for your project.
materialsHub.heroSubtitle: Aluminum or vinyl? Here's everything you need to choose the right material for your Central Florida home — lifespan, cost, hurricane rating, and maintenance.
materialsHub.cardsSubtitle: We install both aluminum and vinyl. Each has a different set of trade-offs.
materialsHub.alumDesc: The industry standard for Florida new construction and replacement. Fire-resistant, hurricane-rated, and maintenance-free for 20–30 years.
materialsHub.vinylDesc: Cost-effective choice for shaded locations and budget-sensitive projects. Good performance under normal Florida conditions.
materialsHub.vinylCon: Can warp in extreme Florida heat (south/west facing)
materialsHub.recAlumItem3: History of pest or animal intrusion
materialsHub.recAlumItem6: Gutters are attached to the fascia
materialsHub.recVinylItem4: Matching existing vinyl on the house
materialsHub.recVinylWarning: Avoid vinyl on south/west-facing soffit in Florida — heat warping risk is real.
materialsHub.faq1Q: What is the best soffit material for Florida?
materialsHub.faq1A: Aluminum is the best choice for most Florida homes. Non-combustible, hurricane-rated, 20–30 year lifespan, no maintenance. Vinyl is acceptable for budget-constrained projects in shaded locations, but it may warp in extreme heat and is not fire-resistant.
materialsHub.faq2Q: How long does aluminum soffit last vs vinyl in Florida?
materialsHub.faq2A: Aluminum lasts 20–30 years in Florida with no maintenance. Vinyl lasts 15–20 years under normal conditions. South-facing vinyl in full Florida sun may start fading and becoming brittle in 10–12 years.
materialsHub.ctaSubtitle: We'll assess your home and recommend the right material for your specific situation. Free estimate, written quote before any work starts.

removeReplace.metaTitle: Remove & Replace Soffit | 1-Story & 2-Story | Orlando | Bubbles Enterprise
removeReplace.metaDesc: Complete soffit and fascia Remove & Replace in Orlando. 1-story and 2-story homes. Aluminum and vinyl. Licensed & Insured. Free estimate.
removeReplace.metaOgTitle: Soffit Remove & Replace | Bubbles Enterprise Orlando
removeReplace.metaOgDesc: Full Remove & Replace soffit installation in Orlando. Old materials removed, new aluminum or vinyl installed. Free estimate.
removeReplace.heroSubtitle: Your old soffit is falling apart, letting in water, animals, and heat. Patching won't cut it anymore. We strip everything off and install a brand-new system — aluminum or vinyl, your call. Done in days, not weeks.
removeReplace.when1Body: It's not one bad panel — it's multiple sections rotted, sagging, or falling apart. When the damage is this widespread, repair is just a Band-Aid. You need a full replacement.
removeReplace.when2Body: Original wood soffit from the 80s or 90s? It's past its lifespan. Wood rots in Florida's humidity. Upgrading to aluminum or vinyl means you won't deal with this again for 20-30 years.
removeReplace.when3Body: If your attic feels like an oven and your AC bill keeps climbing, your soffit probably has no vents — or they're blocked. A full R&R lets us design proper ventilation from scratch.
removeReplace.when4Body: Nothing kills curb appeal faster than old, stained soffit. If you're selling, renovating, or just tired of how it looks — new soffit and fascia transforms the whole exterior instantly.
removeReplace.when5Body: Got a letter from the HOA? Faded, cracked, or damaged soffit is one of the most common violations. A full R&R gets you compliant and keeps them off your back for years.
removeReplace.when6Body: Tired of calling contractors every couple years to patch the same spots? A full Remove & Replace with aluminum gives you 20-30 years of zero maintenance. One job, done right, and you move on.
removeReplace.benefitsTitle: What You Actually Get with a Full R&R
removeReplace.ben1Body: We tear off every piece of old material and install fresh soffit and fascia. Aluminum lasts 20-30 years. Vinyl lasts 10+. Either way, you're set for decades.
removeReplace.ben2Body: We design ventilation into every install. Proper airflow means your attic stays cooler, your AC works less, and your energy bill drops. Most homeowners feel the difference the first month.
removeReplace.ben3Body: No more gaps for water, animals, or insects. A brand-new system means every panel is locked in, every seam is tight, and your roof structure is fully protected.
removeReplace.ben4Body: New soffit and fascia instantly transforms how your house looks from the street. It's one of the highest-impact exterior upgrades you can make — and buyers notice.
removeReplace.ben5Body: Every installation meets current IRC 806 ventilation standards. That means proper airflow, no future code issues, and peace of mind if you ever sell.
removeReplace.energyTitle: Your AC Is Working Overtime Because of Your Soffit
removeReplace.energy1Title: Summer: Your Attic Is an Oven
removeReplace.energy1Body: Without proper soffit ventilation, your attic traps heat like a greenhouse. Your AC runs nonstop trying to compensate. New vented soffit lets hot air escape and drops your cooling costs significantly.
removeReplace.energy1Stat: Significant savings on your summer AC bill
removeReplace.energy2Body: Florida's humidity doesn't stop in winter. Without ventilation, moisture condenses in your attic, soaks your insulation, and breeds mold. Balanced airflow through vented soffit prevents all of this.
removeReplace.energy2Stat: Protects your insulation and roof structure year-round
removeReplace.energy3Title: The Damage You Don't See Yet
removeReplace.energy3Body: Bad soffit doesn't just raise your bills — it rots the wood structure behind it, grows mold, and shortens your roof's lifespan. Replacing now prevents problems that cost many times more to fix later.
removeReplace.energy3Stat: Prevention costs a fraction of emergency repair
removeReplace.highlightTitle: Why Ventilation Is the Real Upgrade
removeReplace.hl1: Vented soffit lets trapped heat escape — your AC finally gets a break
removeReplace.hl2: Condensation disappears, so insulation and wood stay dry
removeReplace.hl3: Wet insulation loses most of its effectiveness — dry insulation does its job
removeReplace.hl4: Less strain on your AC and heater means fewer repairs and a longer system life
removeReplace.hlStatSub: Properly ventilated soffit vs. sealed or damaged systems
removeReplace.ctaTitle: Your Old Soffit Had Its Run. Time for an Upgrade.
removeReplace.ctaSubtitle: New soffit and fascia. Proper ventilation. Lower energy bills. A house that looks brand new from the street. Get a free quote — we'll walk you through exactly what your home needs.
removeReplace.ctaSubtext: Free inspection. No pressure. No obligation.

newConstruction.metaTitle: New Construction Soffit Installation | Orlando, FL | Bubbles Enterprise
newConstruction.metaDesc: Professional soffit and fascia installation for new construction homes in Orlando and Central Florida. IRC Section 806 compliant, optimal ventilation, aluminum and vinyl. Free quote.
newConstruction.metaOgTitle: New Construction Soffit Installation | Bubbles Enterprise Orlando
newConstruction.metaOgDesc: Expert soffit installation for new builds. Building code compliant, energy efficient, coordinated with your construction timeline.
newConstruction.heroSubtitle: Most builders treat soffit as an afterthought. Install it however and hope it passes inspection. We do the opposite: every vent calculated, every panel aligned, every code requirement documented. Specialists, not generalists.
newConstruction.whySubtitle: Because getting it wrong means callbacks, failed inspections, and unhappy homeowners. We get it right the first time.
newConstruction.reason1Body: We design ventilation to IRC Section 806 before touching a single panel. The inspector gets documentation on arrival. No rework, no delays, no excuses.
newConstruction.reason2Body: Balanced intake and exhaust, calculated by attic square footage. Proper airflow prevents moisture, extends roof shingle life by 20-30%, and reduces HVAC costs.
newConstruction.reason3Title: Lower Energy Bills From Day One
newConstruction.reason3Body: When soffit ventilation is designed alongside insulation and HVAC, homeowners see 15-25% cooling cost reduction. In Florida, that's real money.
newConstruction.reason4Body: Aluminum soffit lasts 20-30 years under Florida's sun, heat, and hurricanes. Vinyl is a solid budget option at 10-15 years. We help pick what fits the build and the budget.
newConstruction.reason5Body: Bad soffit comes back to haunt you: mold complaints, pest entry, fallen panels, warranty claims. Do it right the first time with a crew that only does this.
newConstruction.reason6Body: Pre-drywall, post-roofing, between trades. We show up when you need us, work clean, and stay out of the way. Coordination via text or phone, no runaround.
newConstruction.ircDesc: Florida residential code requires balanced ventilation between soffit intake and ridge/gable exhaust. We calculate and document everything.
newConstruction.ircItem1: Min. 1 sq ft per 300 sq ft of attic space
newConstruction.ircItem3: Continuous ventilation preferred over spot vents
newConstruction.ventItem1: Prevents moisture buildup, mold, and wood rot
newConstruction.ventItem2: Reduces cooling costs by 15-25% in Florida homes
newConstruction.ventItem5: May qualify for energy efficiency credits
newConstruction.processSubtitle: You focus on the build. We handle the soffit.
newConstruction.step1Body: Tell us what you're building. Share the blueprint or just the measurements. We take it from there.
newConstruction.step2Body: We calculate IRC 806 ventilation, pick the right material, and prepare inspector documentation. You don't have to think about it.
newConstruction.step3Body: Our crew arrives on your schedule, works clean alongside your other trades, and installs everything to code.
newConstruction.step4Body: We stay until the inspector signs off. You get the warranty paperwork and our number if you ever need us again.
newConstruction.timelineSubtitle: Tell us when. We'll be there.
newConstruction.phase1Item2: Plan ventilation layout before walls close
newConstruction.phase1Item4: Work alongside electrical for soffit lighting
newConstruction.phase2Item4: Align panel colors with paint plan
newConstruction.ctaTitle: Your Next Build Deserves Soffit Done by Specialists.
newConstruction.ctaSubtitle: Send us the blueprints or call (407) 715-1790. We'll prepare the ventilation plan, material quote, and a schedule that fits your build.
newConstruction.ctaSubtext: We only do soffit and fascia. Every build we touch passes inspection the first time.

soffitRepairOrlando.metaTitle: Soffit Repair Orlando — Attic Ventilation & Pest Defense | Bubbles Enterprise
soffitRepairOrlando.metaDesc: Expert soffit repair in Orlando. Fix attic ventilation, lower energy bills, block pests. Storm damage, rot, animal intrusion repairs. Same-day estimates. Licensed & Insured.
soffitRepairOrlando.metaOgDesc: More than just looks — proper soffit is the key to a cooler attic and a pest-free home.
soffitRepairOrlando.heroSubtitle: More than just looks — proper soffit is the key to a cooler attic, lower energy bills, and a pest-free home.
soffitRepairOrlando.whyTitle: Is Your Soffit Doing Its Job?
soffitRepairOrlando.whyDesc: In Florida, soffit isn't just decorative. It's the "lungs" of your house. If your vents are painted over, clogged, or damaged, your home is suffocating.
soffitRepairOrlando.why1Desc: Blocked vents trap heat in your attic, forcing your AC to work harder. Proper airflow can lower cooling costs by 10–15%.
soffitRepairOrlando.why2Desc: Raccoons, squirrels, and wasps love Orlando attics. A single loose panel is an open door. We seal it tight.
soffitRepairOrlando.why3Desc: Florida's tropical storms rip and dislodge panels. We assess, repair, and reinforce before the next storm.
soffitRepairOrlando.std2: Hidden vent profiles for clean looks
soffitRepairOrlando.faq1q: Is soffit repair common in older Orlando homes?
soffitRepairOrlando.faq1a: Yes, extremely. Many older homes in Winter Park and Downtown Orlando have wooden soffits suffering from rot due to Florida's humidity. Upgrading to aluminum or vinyl is a lasting fix.
soffitRepairOrlando.faq2q: Can damaged soffit increase AC costs in Florida?
soffitRepairOrlando.faq2a: Absolutely. Blocked soffit vents trap heat in your attic, forcing your AC to work harder. Proper airflow helps your AC run more efficiently — we've seen 10–15% cooling cost reductions.
soffitRepairOrlando.faq3q: Do animals enter through soffit gaps?
soffitRepairOrlando.faq3a: They sure do. It's the most common entry point for squirrels, rats, and bats in Orlando. We don't just cover the hole — we secure the entire perimeter.
soffitRepairOrlando.ctaSubtitle: Don't wait for mold or pests to move in. Get a professional inspection.

fasciaRepairOrlando.metaTitle: Fascia Repair & Replacement in Orlando, FL | Bubbles Enterprise
fasciaRepairOrlando.metaDesc: Expert fascia repair and replacement in Orlando, FL. Rotted wood fascia, damaged fascia boards, gutter-related fascia damage. Licensed & insured. Free estimates. (407) 715-1790.
fasciaRepairOrlando.metaOgDesc: Fascia protects your roofline from water damage. Expert repair and replacement across Orlando and Central Florida.
fasciaRepairOrlando.heroSubtitle: Your fascia holds your gutters and seals your roofline. When it rots or pulls away, water damage follows fast. We replace it right — aluminum or vinyl, same week.
fasciaRepairOrlando.whatIsFasciaDesc: Fascia is the vertical finishing board that runs along the outer edge of your roofline — right where the roof meets the top of your exterior walls. It's the board your gutters attach to, and it protects your roof rafters from direct weather exposure.
fasciaRepairOrlando.fasciaFact2: Gutter system mounts directly to fascia
fasciaRepairOrlando.fasciaFact3: Seals the roofline edge from moisture
fasciaRepairOrlando.fasciaFact4: Works with soffit to protect your attic
fasciaRepairOrlando.signsSubtitle: Fascia damage worsens fast in Florida's humidity. Catching it early saves thousands in water damage.
fasciaRepairOrlando.sign1Body: Dark staining, soft spots, or visibly rotted wood on your fascia boards. This is the #1 sign in Florida — often caused by gutters overflowing onto the fascia.
fasciaRepairOrlando.sign2Body: Paint peeling off your fascia usually means moisture is getting into the wood underneath. Fix the fascia before repainting — paint alone won't stop the rot.
fasciaRepairOrlando.sign3Body: If your fascia board is pulling away from the roofline or sagging, the wood may be structurally compromised. Your gutters are probably also off-pitch.
fasciaRepairOrlando.sign4Body: Heavy Florida rain loads and clogged gutters create weight and water pressure that physically pull fascia boards away from the roof. Needs immediate attention.
fasciaRepairOrlando.vsTitle: Fascia vs. Soffit — What's the Difference?
fasciaRepairOrlando.processSubtitle: From call to cleanup — usually completed in one day.
fasciaRepairOrlando.proc1Desc: We assess the full fascia perimeter and identify all damage — rot, structural issues, and attachment problems.
fasciaRepairOrlando.proc2Desc: Transparent quote with labor, material, and linear footage before any work begins. No surprises.
fasciaRepairOrlando.proc3Desc: Old fascia removed. Sub-fascia inspected. If structural rot is found, we stop and notify you immediately.
fasciaRepairOrlando.proc4Desc: New aluminum or vinyl fascia installed and secured to your roofline. Color-matched to existing trim.
fasciaRepairOrlando.proc5Desc: Full site cleanup including magnetic nail sweep. You inspect before we leave.
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq1q: What is fascia on a house?
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq1a: Fascia is the vertical board running along the outer edge of your roofline. It's where your gutters attach, and it protects your roof rafters from direct weather exposure.
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq2q: What causes fascia to rot in Orlando?
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq2a: The main culprit in Orlando is overflowing or clogged gutters pushing water back against the wood fascia. Florida's humidity accelerates the rot once moisture gets in.
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq3q: Can you repair fascia without replacing the soffit?
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq3a: Yes — fascia and soffit are separate components. If only the fascia is damaged, we replace just the fascia boards.
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq4q: How much does fascia repair cost in Orlando?
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq4a: Fascia replacement in Orlando runs $6–$12 per linear foot installed. A typical single-story home with 150–200 LF runs $900–$2,400 for fascia alone.
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq5q: Do you reinstall gutters after fascia replacement?
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq5a: We do not reinstall old gutters — they warp and deform on removal. You'll need a gutter company for new gutters after our work.
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq6q: What is the difference between soffit and fascia?
fasciaRepairOrlando.faq6a: Soffit is horizontal — on the underside of your roof overhang. Fascia is vertical — at the front edge of the roofline. They work together and are usually replaced together.
fasciaRepairOrlando.areasSubtitle: Fascia repair and replacement in Orlando and surrounding areas
fasciaRepairOrlando.ctaSubtitle: Free on-site inspection. Written estimate before any work starts. Most fascia jobs completed same week.
